title: gstack — Workflow Skills for Claude Code
description: A guided tour of gstack: six opinionated workflow skills that turn Claude Code from one generic assistant into a team of specialists. Covers the skill system, the browse engine, CLI architecture, and testing.

steps.title: Welcome to gstack
steps.description: gstack is a collection of six workflow skills for Claude Code — plan review, code review, shipping, browser automation, and retrospectives. Each skill gives the agent a different cognitive mode: founder taste, engineering rigor, paranoid reviewer, release engineer, QA tester, or engineering manager. The project is organized with one directory per skill and a core headless browser engine in browse/.

steps.title: The Skill System
steps.description: Each skill is defined by a SKILL.md file — a Markdown prompt that Claude Code discovers and executes as a slash command. The six skills are: /plan-ceo-review (founder mode), /plan-eng-review (eng manager mode), /review (paranoid staff engineer), /ship (release engineer), /browse (QA engineer via headless Chromium), and /retro (engineering manager retrospectives). The root SKILL.md defines the browse skill entry point.

steps.title: The Browse Engine
steps.description: The core technical component — a compiled headless Chromium CLI built on Playwright. It runs a persistent browser daemon on localhost (port 9400-9410) with bearer token auth. First call starts the server (~3s), every subsequent call runs in ~100-200ms. The browser state (cookies, tabs, localStorage) persists between commands and auto-shuts down after 30 minutes idle.

steps.title: CLI & Server Architecture
steps.description: The browse CLI is split into focused modules: cli.ts is the entry point and command router, server.ts manages the persistent Chromium daemon, and browser-manager.ts handles Playwright lifecycle. Commands are organized by concern — read-commands.ts (text, html, links, snapshot), write-commands.ts (click, fill, type), meta-commands.ts (tabs, viewport, cookies), and snapshot.ts builds the accessibility tree with @ref-based element selection.

steps.title: Integration Tests
steps.description: Tests live in browse/test/ and use a local test server (test-server.ts) with HTML fixtures to exercise the full CLI. commands.test.ts covers the command surface — navigation, content extraction, interaction, and inspection. snapshot.test.ts validates the accessibility tree and @ref-based selection system. Run tests with `bun test`.

steps.title: Setup & Configuration
steps.description: The setup script handles one-time installation: it runs `bun install`, compiles the browse binary to browse/dist/browse (~58MB), and creates symlinks so Claude Code discovers each skill. package.json defines the build scripts. CLAUDE.md provides development instructions. The compiled binary and node_modules are gitignored — teammates just run `./setup` once after cloning.
